name: CALM 1.2 Controls Reference
description: A reference architecture demonstrating controls at three attachment scopes in CALM 1.2: top-level (system-wide policies), per-node (data classification on a specific node), and per-relationship (mTLS enforcement on a specific connection). The scenario is a KYC (Know Your Customer) compliance service used in FSI onboarding workflows.

encryption-in-transit.description: All data transmitted between system components must be encrypted using TLS 1.3 or higher with validated certificates. This control applies system-wide to every connection in this architecture.

nodes.name: Compliance Officer
nodes.description: Human reviewer responsible for approving KYC checks and monitoring compliance status.

nodes.name: KYC Firewall
nodes.description: Network perimeter control that restricts inbound traffic to authorised sources and enforces mTLS for internal service calls.

nodes.name: KYC Service
nodes.description: Backend service that executes identity verification checks, risk scoring, and regulatory lookups for customer onboarding.

nodes.name: KYC Records Database
nodes.description: Persistent store for KYC verification results, identity documents, and audit trails. Contains PII and is subject to GDPR and FCA data retention rules.

data-classification.description: KYC records contain Personally Identifiable Information (PII) subject to GDPR Article 5 retention limitations and FCA SYSC 9.1 recordkeeping requirements. Data must be classified and retention enforced.

relationships.description: Compliance officer reviews KYC results and triggers manual overrides via the KYC service interface.
The KYC firewall forwards validated requests to the KYC service using mutual TLS. This relationship carries a per-relationship control enforcing certificate rotation.

mutual-tls.description: Both the firewall and the KYC service must present valid certificates for this connection. Certificates must be rotated regularly to limit the blast radius of a key compromise.

relationships.description: KYC service reads and writes verification results and identity records to the KYC records database via JDBC.
